=== IPG Job Employee Job Descriptions (1).pdf ===
**Job Title: Owner/CEO**
**Employee: Dondi Sanchez**
**Years with IPG: Since 2005**
**Responsibilities:**
- Oversee and manage all daily operations of IPG.
- Engage with clients, provide project bids, and maintain client relationships.
- Lead marketing efforts to promote IPG's services.
- Serve as the Director of Photography for select projects.
- Operate cameras for various productions.
- Perform steadicam operation as needed.
- Personally select and take on specific job assignments.
- Manage all equipment purchasing for the company.
**Qualifications:**
- Founding member of IPG with extensive experience since 2005.
- Strong leadership and management skills.
- Effective communication and client relationship management.
- Proficiency in Director of Photography, camera operation, and steadicam operation.
- Demonstrated ability to choose and execute various job assignments.
- Expertise in equipment procurement and maintenance.
This job description outlines Dondi Sanchez's multifaceted role as the Owner/CEO, reflecting
their contributions to the company's success and the diverse responsibilities they handle.
**Job Title: Sound Mixer/Camera Assistant/Grip/Web Stream Operator**
**Employee: Eli Pena**
**Years with IPG: 7 years**
**Responsibilities:**
- Operate and maintain sound devices such as Sound Device 664, 633, and 442.
- Serve as a camera assistant, including pulling focus and occasionally operating the camera for
corporate events.
- Manage web streaming devices, including Vmix and Atem Mini.
- Set up lighting and grip equipment.
- Maintain and update the IPG Rentals website.
- Provide excellent client service and communication.
**Qualifications:**
- 7 years of experience with IPG.
- Proficiency in sound mixing, camera operation, and web streaming.
- Strong technical skills in setting up lighting and grip equipment.
- Excellent interpersonal skills for client interactions.

- Ability to work effectively in a team environment.
---
**Job Title: Editor/Photoshop Expert/Camera Operator/Equipment Technician**
**Employee: Ray Combs**
**Years with IPG: Since September 2019**
**Responsibilities:**
- Expertise in video editing and Photoshop.
- Strong client communication and relationship management.
- Proficiency in camera operation and equipment repair.
- Technical competence in engineering multi-camera switch systems and operating web
streaming devices.
**Qualifications:**
- Extensive experience in video production and broadcast networks.
- Exceptional editing and Photoshop skills.
- Strong client interaction and communication abilities.
- Technical proficiency in camera operation and equipment repair.
- Knowledge of multi-camera switch systems and web streaming devices.
**Job Title: Director of Photography/Camera Operator/Lighting Specialist/Equipment
Technician**
**Employee: Ben Lowrey**
**Years with IPG: Since the end of 2022**
**Responsibilities:**
- Director of Photography and camera operation for high-level corporate videos and TV
production.
- Expertise in lighting design and implementation.
- Proficiency in steadicam operation.
- Equipment repair and maintenance.
**Qualifications:**
- Over 27 years of industry experience.
- Proven track record in corporate video and TV production.
- Exceptional lighting skills.
- Steadicam operation expertise.
- Equipment repair and maintenance capabilities.

**Job Title: Assistant Camera/Grip/Computer Specialist/Sound Mixer**
**Employee: Tyler Hailey**
**Years with IPG: Since the beginning of 2023**
**Responsibilities:**
- Serve as an assistant camera operator.
- Work as a grip for various production needs.
- Handle computer-related tasks.
- Learn and perform sound mixing duties.
- Collaborate effectively as a team player.
**Qualifications:**
- 8 years of industry experience.
- Proficiency in assistant camera work and grip responsibilities.
- Strong computer skills, including 3D printing.
- Willingness to learn and adapt, including sound mixing.
- Excellent teamwork and dedication.

effort, the non-performing party is unable in whole or in part to prevent or overcome. The “Force Majeure” may cause travel restrictions, self-imposed
and/or governmental required isolations, potential closures of offices and institutions required to perform due diligence, fund and close. All time periods,
including Closing, will be extended for a reasonable amount of time after the Force Majeure no longer prevents performance under this Agreement,
